h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Twelve sacks instead of thirty makes a shortage possible, but it does not make £1.40 innocent.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange was heading toward placing individual pieces of evidence on the board; L3 rejects the piecemeal approach and demands a relational, simultaneous public display.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [pins a new rule across the price board: CONSTRAINT IS A CLAIM, NOT A DEFENCE.] Then the word constraint gets no free pass. [draws a row beneath the four headings: STOCK / DELIVERY COST / MARGIN / SHORTAGE RULE]
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After L2 exposes 'constraint' as a respectable word for the same squeeze, the tutor abandons the prior framework by publicly demoting constraint from an explanatory category to a claim, then re-orients the entire board around a relational margin formula.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: So take the missing account line. Which number has to be made public first: delivered cost per sack, margin per kilo, or the rationing rule?
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor takes up L3’s insistence on public, simultaneous visibility of cost and rule, transforming the question from evidence-placement into a design choice about public account lines.
